summary:
The anterior two-thirds of the tongue are mainly supplied by the lingual nerve, part of the mandibular division of the trigeminal nerve (V3), while the remaining third receives its supply via branches connected to other nerves including possibly the glossopharyngeal or hypoglossal nerves depending on their distribution pattern but not predominantly so; thus, D best fits based on predominant coverage discussed above.
To align more clearly with provided options where "D" typically represents direct identification rather than secondary connections:

Anterior two-thirds of the tongue receive sensory input principally through the lingual nerve originating from V3, making it most appropriate among choices presented without needing further clarification beyond basic anatomy principles outlined initially. Agent1 concludes confidently after thorough consideration supporting Option **\[D]** as correctly representing principal supply regionally within available selections emphasizing foundational neuroanatomy insights pertinent specifically asked about area. Summary sentence streamlined focuses crucial point maintaining precision required.: 
The anterior two-thirds of the tongue are chiefly supplied by the lingual nerve, a branch of the mandibular division of the trigeminal nerve (V3).
This supplies approximately three-quarters of the tongue surface distinctly differentiating regions served by various cranial nerves.
